title: Malian Forces Briefly Enter Mauritanian Territory in Border Incident
detail: Mauritanian media reported that Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) units briefly crossed into Mauritanian territory and occupied six villages before withdrawing following diplomatic negotiations. The incident was attributed to a GPS error or border demarcation confusion rather than deliberate aggression; no combat occurred. FAMa withdrew after the situation was resolved through direct communications between Malian and Mauritanian military commanders. The incident highlights ongoing border security challenges along the Mali-Mauritania frontier, where JNIM has expanded westward through Kayes Region toward Mauritanian territory. Mauritania has maintained border fortification and counter-terrorism operations to prevent jihadist spillover from Mali.
